incorrects.description: Student selected B: 2 bags of 4, factor reversal error

steps.text: The first number tells you how many groups. Look at the first number in 4 times 2. How many groups do you need?

incorrects.description: Student selected C: 6 scattered items, not equal groups

steps.text: Multiplication shows equal groups. The expression tells you how many groups and how many in each. Which picture shows groups?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Option A bags highlight one at a time, then items in one bag highlight.

steps.text: Let me show you. The expression 4 times 2 means 4 groups with 2 in each group. I count the groups: 1, 2, 3, 4 bags. Then I check inside one bag: 2 items. Option A shows 4 groups of 2. That's what the expression means.
